title: The Hive
description: The Hive is a fiction by Camilo José Cela. Banned by censors of Francoist Spain.
descriptionParagraphs: The Hive is a fiction by Camilo José Cela. Banned by censors of Francoist Spain.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a fiction, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Spain. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned by censors of Francoist Spain. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: The Hive entered censorship debates as a fiction associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
